text:
**IBEW Traveler Snapshot — Monday, July 6, 2026**

**LU-481 Indianapolis, IN** — Top scale hits **$54.75**, and Meade Electric is running 30 hands on the Meta Data Center at **$53.80 with 10% over-scale** on a grinding 5-12 Mon-Fri plus Saturday schedule. That's serious money for serious hours. Google Data Center work through Ermco and Eli Lilly LP1 round out 38 total calls for 187 hands—solid depth with decent variety beyond the data center grind.

**LU-494 Milwaukee, WI** — Port Washington Data Center is on a REPOST rampage: four contractors (MJ Electric, Gurtz, Cupertino, Uihlein) stacking 63 calls needing hands at **JW scale plus $10/hr** across 40 total openings. That's **$10/hour premium** for solid work—Wisconsin license required on most. Plenty of board depth at 169 hands needed.

**LU-613 Atlanta, GA** — **$48.00 scale** anchors 36 calls. Inglett & Stubbs dominates with QTS Fayette and Wellstar Paulding Hospital work; Miller Electrical is hunting cable splicers at **$40.90** with documented experience requirements. Beltline's Yates plant expansion at **$42.18** adds heavy-duty options. Hands needed: 213.

**LU-124 Kansas City, MO** — Google NRD is eating tickets for Capital and Mark One (64 combined calls) across 34 total openings with standard Inside JW rates. Background checks and OSHA 10 are table stakes, but the board's deep at 188 hands and work is steady.

**LU-405 Cedar Rapids, IA** — Data center boom continues: EWD and QTS are pulling 50+ combined calls for Inside JW hands at Iowa scale. Expect OSHA 10/30 and license requirements across the 26-call board serving 134 hands.

Data centers remain the economic bloodhound of 2026—if you're chasing premium hours and willing to relocate, they're printing money across the upper Midwest.
